projectSummary: PRD v2.1 outlines a Social Media Integration feature (priority: High, owner: Development Team, timeline: 3 sprints / 6 weeks) bundling three user stories — share content to Twitter, log in with Google/Facebook, and an admin moderation workflow for shared content — on top of an OAuth 2.0 integration backbone, a 100 req/hour rate limit, and a third-party content moderation API. The PRD explicitly names API rate limits and security compliance as known risks.
objectives: Ship share-to-Twitter for user-generated content within the 6-week window
Ship Google and Facebook social login on top of OAuth 2.0
Deliver an admin moderation workflow for shared content powered by the moderation API
Enforce a 100 requests/hour per-user rate limit across the social integration surface
Pass a security/compliance review covering OAuth and social-data handling before launch
Deliver all three user stories within the 3-sprint (6-week) timeline

tasks.name: Implement OAuth 2.0 integration layer (token store, consent UX, refresh handling)
Implement Google login via OAuth 2.0 (user story: login with Google)
Implement Facebook login via OAuth 2.0 (user story: login with Facebook)
Build share-to-Twitter feature for user-generated content
Integrate third-party content moderation API into the share pipeline
Build admin moderation dashboard/workflow for shared content
Implement 100 requests/hour rate limiting across the social integration surface
Run security/compliance review covering OAuth tokens, social data, and privacy
Define acceptance criteria and MVP cut for each of the three user stories

risks.name: Social platform API rate limits could throttle user sharing and login volume
risks.description: Social platform API rate limits could throttle user sharing and login volume
risks.solution: Implement the 100 req/hour limit on our side (token-bucket per user/IP), add automated alerting when upstream providers (Twitter, Google, Facebook) return 429s, and hold a vendor-quota review per provider before each sprint so we can request higher caps in advance.

risks.name: Security and compliance gaps (OAuth token handling, social-data privacy) could block launch
risks.description: Security and compliance gaps (OAuth token handling, social-data privacy) could block launch
risks.solution: Schedule a security/compliance review in sprint 1, use a vetted OAuth 2.0 library, encrypt tokens at rest, run a threat model for social-data flows (GDPR/CCPA), and gate the final ship on the review's sign-off.

risks.name: Twitter, Google, or Facebook API policy or SDK changes could silently break integration
risks.description: Twitter, Google, or Facebook API policy or SDK changes could silently break integration
risks.solution: Pin SDK versions, add a vendor-changelog watch, wrap third-party calls in an abstraction layer so a provider migration only touches one module, and add contract tests against the live APIs in CI to catch breakage early.

risks.name: Content moderation API may produce false positives or false negatives, harming UX
risks.description: Content moderation API may produce false positives or false negatives, harming UX
risks.solution: Tune moderation thresholds from real labelled samples before launch, surface an admin override on the moderation dashboard, log all moderation decisions for review, and design the user-facing UX so borderline content is held rather than auto-blocked.

risks.name: 6-week (3 sprint) timeline is tight for three user stories plus OAuth, rate limiting, and a moderation API
risks.description: 6-week (3 sprint) timeline is tight for three user stories plus OAuth, rate limiting, and a moderation API
risks.solution: Pre-agree an MVP cut per sub-feature (share, login, moderation) per sprint; front-load the OAuth 2.0 spike so platform work unblocks parallel streams; track capacity daily and lock a Friday go/no-go before the final sprint.

risks.name: No named individual owner (PRD names only 'Development Team') creates an accountability gap
risks.description: No named individual owner (PRD names only 'Development Team') creates an accountability gap
risks.solution: Assign a single DRI per workstream (Twitter share, Google login, Facebook login, moderation, rate limiting, security review) before sprint 1 kickoff so each stream has a clear escalation path.

recommendations.title: Front-load an OAuth 2.0 spike covering Google, Facebook, and Twitter in sprint 1 so token storage, refresh, and consent UX are de-risked before downstream work.
recommendations.description: Front-load an OAuth 2.0 spike covering Google, Facebook, and Twitter in sprint 1 so token storage, refresh, and consent UX are de-risked before downstream work.

recommendations.title: Pre-agree an MVP cut for each sub-feature (share, login, moderation) per sprint so a slip on one stream does not cascade across the 6-week window.
recommendations.description: Pre-agree an MVP cut for each sub-feature (share, login, moderation) per sprint so a slip on one stream does not cascade across the 6-week window.

recommendations.title: Lock the content moderation API contract and admin UX in sprint 1 so the moderation dashboard does not need to be rebuilt after backend changes.
recommendations.description: Lock the content moderation API contract and admin UX in sprint 1 so the moderation dashboard does not need to be rebuilt after backend changes.

recommendations.title: Add a load test for the 100 req/hour rate limiter early in QA, not at the end, and instrument per-user counters so abuse is visible in production.
recommendations.description: Add a load test for the 100 req/hour rate limiter early in QA, not at the end, and instrument per-user counters so abuse is visible in production.

recommendations.title: Schedule the security/compliance review before the final sprint so any OAuth or data-handling remediation can fit inside the timeline.
recommendations.description: Schedule the security/compliance review before the final sprint so any OAuth or data-handling remediation can fit inside the timeline.

recommendations.title: Assign a named DRI per workstream under 'Development Team' before sprint 1 so each stream has a clear escalation path.
recommendations.description: Assign a named DRI per workstream under 'Development Team' before sprint 1 so each stream has a clear escalation path.
